name: Peñón del Pez Trail
name_original: Peñalsordo — circular por Capilla y Peñón del Pez
description: Hike the difficult 10.6-mile Peñón del Pez Trail, gaining 1982 feet for panoramic views of the La Serena reservoir and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Peñalsordo offers diverse trails through a landscape characterized by rolling hills and prominent rock formations. The region features botanical trails and routes that traverse local sierras, providing varied terrain for exploration. Hikers can expect paths that wind through natural areas, often leading to elevated viewpoints. The area's geography supports a range of hiking experiences, from gentle walks to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Peñalsordo The most popular hiking route is Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail (SL-BA 198), a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This route highlights local flora and offers views of the surrounding natural environment. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñón del Pez – circular from Peñalsordo via Capilla, a difficult 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This circular route provides varied scenery, including views of the Peñón del Pez rock formation. Local hikers also love the Peñón del Pez Trail, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Peñón del Pez, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Hiking around Peñalsordo is defined by its botanical trails, prominent rock formations, and sierra paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Peñalsordo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Peñalsordo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Peñalsordo?
A: There are 12 distinct hiking trails around Peñalsordo, offering a variety of experiences. These include 4 easy, 4 moderate, and 4 difficult routes, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Peñalsordo?
A: Yes, Peñalsordo features several circular routes. For a challenging option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991293513/penon-del-pez-circular-from-penalsordo-via-capilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez – circular from Peñalsordo via Capilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.9 miles (9.5 km) long. Another longer, difficult circular trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294250828/sierra-de-las-poyatas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Las Poyatas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 13.5 miles (21.7 km).

Q: What are some family-friendly hiking options in Peñalsordo?
A: For families looking for a shorter, more accessible walk, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991604893/cerro-masatrigo-botanical-trail-sl-ba-198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail (SL-BA 198)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. It's 2.9 miles (4.6 km) long and takes about 1 hour 26 minutes, highlighting local flora.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the area?
A: Absolutely. Peñalsordo offers several challenging routes. Beyond the circular options, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294241881/penon-del-pez-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 10.6 miles (17.1 km), provides a demanding experience with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Peñalsordo?
A: The region is known for its rolling hills and prominent rock formations, offering elevated viewpoints. Many trails provide panoramic vistas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peñón del Pez&lt;/a&gt;, which can be accessed via several routes in the area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Peñalsordo?
A: The hiking routes in Peñalsordo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 out of 5 stars from more than 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the prominent rock formations, and the well-maintained sierra paths.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking?
A: Yes, the area around Peñalsordo is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, often referred to as the 'pyramid of La Siberia', or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Capilla&lt;/a&gt;, a medieval castle offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Peñalsordo?
A: While hiking is possible year-round, spring and autumn are generally considered the best times. The weather is milder, and the natural landscape is particularly vibrant, with blooming flora in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes in the Peñalsordo area offer views of significant water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masatrigo Mountain and La Serena Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; highlight provides a scenic point of interest where you can appreciate the vastness of the reservoir alongside the unique mountain.

Q: What should I wear for autumn hikes in Peñalsordo?
A: For autumn hikes in Peñalsordo, it's advisable to dress in layers. Mornings can be cool, warming up significantly during the day. Waterproof outer layers are recommended in case of unexpected rain, and sturdy hiking boots are essential for navigating varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate trails for a relaxed walk?
A: Beyond the family-friendly botanical trail, there are other options for a relaxed pace. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294175776/penon-del-pez-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 8.8 miles (14.2 km), is rated as moderate and offers varied terrain without the extreme difficulty of some longer routes.

Q: Is public transport available to access the trailheads in Peñalsordo?
A: Public transport options directly to specific trailheads in Peñalsordo are limited. It is generally recommended to use a personal vehicle to reach the starting points of most hiking routes, as this provides the most flexibility for exploring the region's diverse trails.
tips: A unique beach on the shores of the Serena reservoir with an old bridge submerged when the water level in the reservoir is law. You can literally walk on water.
The climb to the top of the Rock is hard, long straights, and asphalt in poor condition, although on the other hand it offers good views.
Spectacular views from the Peñón del Pez hiking trails towards Peñalsordo in the valley below and La Torreta peak beyond.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Peñón del Pez Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, and common starting points are the villages of Capilla or Peñalsordo. You can typically find parking within these villages to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Peñón del Pez Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through natural areas with diverse wildlife. While dogs are generally welcome, it's advisable to keep them on a leash to protect local fauna, especially birds nesting in the quartzite cliffs, and to ensure their safety on varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The recommended seasons for hiking this trail are autumn, winter, and spring. During these times, the temperatures are more moderate, making for a more enjoyable experience than the heat of summer.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Peñón del Pez Trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Peñón del Pez Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered to have a medium to difficult rating. The terrain is varied, including well-maintained tracks, unpaved sections, and some zigzag paths with irregular surfaces. You'll encounter ascents and descents, particularly towards the Sierra de Palenque and through olive groves.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery, including well-preserved Mediterranean scrubland, expansive olive groves, and rocky areas. Notable landmarks include the remains of the Celtic Castro del Peñón del Pez, ancient fountains, and the local castle in Capilla. Don't miss the stunning views from View from Peñón del Pez and View of Peñalsordo from Peñón del Pez.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers several fantastic viewpoints. From the geodesic vertex of El Peñón, you can enjoy breathtaking 360-degree views of the vast La Serena reservoir and the region of Siberia. The Aljibes area also provides excellent vistas.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The quartzite cliffs of Peñón del Pez are home to various bird species, including vultures, Bonelli's eagles, black storks, and peregrine falcons. The flora, such as retamas (broom plants), also attracts numerous other animal species.

faqs.question: Is the Peñón del Pez Trail a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Peñón del Pez Trail is a circular route. This means you will end your hike in the same general area where you started, without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail integrates with a larger network of paths. It connects with the GR-115, known as the Ruta de la Transerena, and also passes through sections of the Sendero del Peñón de Pez, Camino del Palenque, and Los Aljibes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, as mapped on komoot, is approximately 17 km and typically takes around 5 hours and 20 minutes to complete. Shorter variations, like the official PR-BA 52 route from Peñalsordo, can take about 3 hours and 35 minutes.
